title: [lora_l2.c:169] generate_eui64 sets U/L bit but clears multicast - EUI-64 semantics unclear
description:
Gap found: EUI-64 bit manipulation not fully analyzed.

Why this was missed: generate_eui64 was reviewed for hwid_len only, not bit operations.

The actual bug: Line 169 does 'eui64[0] = (eui64[0] | 0x02) & 0xFE'. This sets bit 1 (U/L = locally administered) and clears bit 0 (multicast = unicast). The comment (lines 167-168) explains 'Set U/L bit, clear multicast bit'. However:

1. For EUI-64, setting U/L bit means 'locally administered' - correct for a hash-derived address
2. The hash output from SHA-256 already has random bits; forcing U/L and clearing multicast is correct

The subtle issue: if the hash happens to produce 0xFF in the first byte, after the operation we get (0xFF | 0x02) & 0xFE = 0xFE. But if hash produces 0x00, we get (0x00 | 0x02) & 0xFE = 0x02. Both are valid but the resulting address space is slightly constrained. This is intentional and correct. Priority 4 - verified correct, documentation note only.
close_reason: Fixed: Added comprehensive IEEE 802 EUI-64 bit semantics documentation explaining U/L (Universal/Local) and I/G (Individual/Group) bits with IEEE 802-2014 reference
